title: Dubyaea gombalana
taxonomy: Dubyaea gombalana is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a type of flowering plant commonly found in the Himalayan region. The species is further categorized as a part of the tribe Cardueae, within the subfamily Carduoideae. It is also referred to as a close relative of the genus Echinops and other related species. The classification of Dubyaea gombalana is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.

If we consider a similar plant then "Dubaea" genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, 

The genus Dubaea comprises flowering plants with medicinal properties. The health benefits of Dubaea include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dubaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dubaea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
